data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Here are all the legacy patterns I found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 4 | `Configuration`/`Platform` conditional defaults | SDK defaults handle this |
| 5 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed in SDK-style |
| 6 | `FileAlignment` | Default, removable |
| 7 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Default for applicable project types |
| 8 | `Deterministic` | Default `true` in SDK-style |
| 9 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (lines 16–31) | SDK provides identical defaults |
| 10 | Explicit framework references (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | Implicit in SDK-style |
| 11 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK-style glob includes all `*.cs` automatically |
| 12 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | SDK auto-generates assembly attributes; metadata goes in `.csproj` properties |
| 13 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` `v4.7.2` | Use `TargetFramework` with TFM `net472` |
| 14 | `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` matching project name | Defaults in SDK-style, can be omitted |
| 15 | `OutputType` `Library` | Default for SDK-style, can be omitted |

Let me apply all of these now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** (line 3) — handled automatically by the SDK
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** (line 46) — handled automatically by the SDK
4. **`Configuration` and `Platform` conditional properties** (lines 5-6) — SDK defaults handle this


5. **`ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, and `Deterministic`** — all either unnecessary or defaulted in SDK-style
6. **Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups** — the SDK provides sensible defaults for debug symbols, optimization levels, output paths, and warning levels
7. **Explicit framework assembly references** like System and System.Core — these are implicit in the SDK
8. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style uses globbing automatically
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — the SDK auto-generates assembly attributes, so I can move that metadata directly into the `.csproj`
10. **`TargetFrameworkVersion`** — needs to become `TargetFramework` with the TFM `net472`
11. **`RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName`** — these default to the project name in SDK-style and can be removed

So my approach is to create a new SDK-style `.csproj`, move the assembly metadata into it as properties, delete the old `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` file, and leave the source files untouched.

Writing the modernized csproj...

Since `OutputType` defaults to `Library` and both `RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName` match the project name `LegacyApp`, I can omit those and keep the file minimal.

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines to 11. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (all implicit in SDK-style):**
- XML declaration, `ToolsVersion`, namespace
- `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports
- `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic`
- `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults, Debug/Release property groups
- `OutputType` (Library is default), `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` (match project name)
- All `<Reference>` items (framework assemblies are implicit)
- All `<Compile>` items (SDK globs `**/*.cs` automatically)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` as `Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`)

**Kept:**
- `TargetFramework` (`net472`) — required
- Assembly metadata — now as MSBuild properties
